---
title: "01_ST_filter_and_integrate.en.Rmd"
author: "Chuhao Li"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

ST Filtering, Integration, and Brain Region Annotation

Processing of spatial transcriptomics data using Giotto v3.1 [@dries2021].

## Loading Data

- Convert matrix data into a Giotto object
- Remove spots not in tissue
- Data normalization: `normalizeGiotto(scalefactor = 6000)`
- The image of batch2 is misaligned. To facilitate subsequent visualization, the spots of batch2 were mirror-flipped and rotated to an appropriate angle.

```{r eval=FALSE, attr.source='.numberLines'}
library(Giotto)
save_dir <- "001Giotto_out/"
# testcombo <- readRDS(paste0(save_dir, "testcombo.rds"))
instrs = createGiottoInstructions(save_dir = save_dir,
                                  save_plot = TRUE,
                                  show_plot = TRUE,
                                  python_path = "/root/.local/share/r-miniconda/envs/giotto_env/bin/python")
gobject_list <- list()
sample.name <- c("PLX5622", "FADCtrl", "WTCtrl")
sample.id.1 <- c("7", "21", "22")
sample.id.2 <- c("AD_PLX5622", "AD_ctrl", "WT_ctrl")
# the order of spatialPlot is control by unique(pDataDT(obj)$list_ID. )
# So, If I want to change the subplot order in spatialPlot,
# I need to change the order here.
for(i in 1:length(sample.name)){
  for (b in as.character(1:2)){
    s.name <- sample.name[i]
    print(paste("Loading", b, s.name, "..."))
    if(b == "1"){
      s.id <- sample.id.1[i]
      s.dir <- paste0("../000data/batch1/sample", s.id)
    } else if(b == "2"){
      s.id <- sample.id.2[i]
      s.dir <- paste0("../000data/batch2/", s.id, "/outs/")
    }
    gobject_list[[paste0(s.name, "_", b)]] <- createGiottoVisiumObject(
      visium_dir = s.dir,
      expr_data = 'filter',
      gene_column_index = 2,
      instructions = instrs,
    )
  }
}
#---------------------#
# join giotto objects #
#---------------------#
# The "join method" influence relative position between image and spots in the spatial plot.
# If you don't use "Shift" in the join method, the resulting spatial plot will not
# show correctly(Images will be overlapped and spots will be separated).
testcombo = joinGiottoObjects(gobject_list = gobject_list,
                              gobject_names = names(gobject_list),
                              join_method = 'shift')
metadata = pDataDT(testcombo)
in_tissue_barcodes = metadata[in_tissue == 1]$cell_ID
testcombo = subsetGiotto(testcombo, cell_ids = in_tissue_barcodes)
testcombo <- normalizeGiotto(gobject = testcombo, scalefactor = 6000)
# giotto.expr.raw <- testcombo@expression$cell$rna$raw
# giotto.expr.normalized == log2(giotto.expr.raw[, 1]*scalefactor/sum(giotto.expr.raw) + 1)
# Each cell is normalized separately, so after integration, normalization is no longer needed.

## add gene & cell statistics
testcombo <- addStatistics(gobject = testcombo)
#-----------------------------------#
# Adjust image position: rotate misaligned images to the correct orientation #
#-----------------------------------#
## visualize
#fDataDT(testcombo)
source("script/utils/rotate_location.r")
source("script/utils/spatialPlot2.r")
testcombo <- do.rotate(testcombo)
expr <- get_expression_values(testcombo)
testcombo@cell_metadata$cell$rna@metaDT$nCount_RNA <- colSums(expr@exprMat)
```

## Data Filtering

Spots were filtered using the `Giotto::filterGiotto` method with the following parameters:
- `expression_threshold = 0.5`
- `feat_det_in_min_cells = 20`
- `min_det_feats_per_cell = 10`

```{r eval=FALSE}
#----------#
# Data Filtering #
#----------#
# Statistics before filtering
# Output: 001preprocess/data_filter_result.csv
#
# nr_feats, spot_count, number of genes? Giotto also seems to filter genes.
source("script/utils/vlnBoxPlot.r")
# Count gene number
for (s in c("AD_ctrl", "AD_PLX5622", "WT_ctrl")){
  cat("gene number of sample:", s, "\n")
  system(paste0("zcat ../000data/batch2/", s,
                "/outs/filtered_feature_bc_matrix/features.tsv.gz |wc -l"))
}
# All are 32285
mean_nr_feats <- aggregate(nr_feats~list_ID, FUN=mean, data=metadata)
spot_count <- aggregate(nr_feats~list_ID, FUN=length, data=metadata)
basic_stat.before_filt <- merge(mean_nr_feats, spot_count, by="list_ID")
names(basic_stat.before_filt) <- c("sample", "mean_nr_feats", "mean_spot_count")
write.csv(basic_stat.before_filt, "001Giotto_out/001preprocess/stat.before_filt.csv")
vlnBoxPlot(testcombo, meta.value="nr_feats", save_path="001Giotto_out/001preprocess/001nr_feats.vlnBox.before_filter.png")
vlnBoxPlot(testcombo, meta.value="nCount_RNA", save_path="001Giotto_out/001preprocess/001nCount_RNA.vlnBox.before_filter.png")
spatialPlot2(testcombo, meta.value="nr_feats",
             save_path="001Giotto_out/001preprocess/001nr_feats.before_filter.png")
spatialPlot2(testcombo, meta.value="nCount_RNA",
             save_path="001Giotto_out/001preprocess/001nCount_RNA.before_filter.png")

## filter
testcombo <- filterGiotto(gobject = testcombo,
                          expression_threshold = 0.5,
                          feat_det_in_min_cells = 20,
                          min_det_feats_per_cell = 10)

# Investigate the proportion of ribosomal and mitochondrial genes
mitochondrial_genes <- grep('mt-', testcombo@feat_ID$rna, value=T)
testcombo <- addFeatsPerc(testcombo, genes=mitochondrial_genes, vector_name="percent.mt")
mitochondrion <- get_genelists_by_go("GO:0005739")
mitochondrion.filt <- intersect(mitochondrion[[1]], testcombo@feat_ID$rna)
testcombo <- addFeatsPerc(testcombo, feats=mitochondrion.filt, vector_name="percent.mt.full")
meta <- pDataDT(testcombo)
summary(meta$percent.mt.full)
# Min. 1st Qu. Median Mean 3rd Qu. Max.
# 15.60 17.60 18.17 18.31 18.89 30.95

ribosome <- get_genelists_by_go("GO:0005840")
ribosome.filt <- intersect(ribosome[[1]], testcombo@feat_ID$rna)
testcombo <- addFeatsPerc(testcombo, feats=ribosome.filt, vector_name="percent.ribo")
meta <- pDataDT(testcombo)
summary(meta$percent.ribo)
# Min. 1st Qu. Median Mean 3rd Qu. Max.
# 4.500 6.110 6.620 6.852 7.290 20.360

# No one recommends removing ribosomal or mitochondrial genes: https://www.biostars.org/p/355658/
# People usually only use mitochondrial genes to infer whether cells are aging and then remove them.
# Extremely lowly expressed genes are also removed.

# Statistics after filtering
metadata = pDataDT(testcombo)
nrow(testcombo@expression$cell$rna$normalized@exprMat) # 18551
mean_nr_feats <- aggregate(nr_feats~list_ID, FUN=mean, data=metadata)
spot_count <- aggregate(nr_feats~list_ID, FUN=length, data=metadata)
basic_stat.after_filt <- merge(mean_nr_feats, spot_count, by="list_ID")
names(basic_stat.after_filt) <- c("sample", "mean_nr_feats.filtered", "mean_spot_count.filtered")
basic_stat.before_filt <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/001preprocess/stat.before_filt.csv", row.names=1)
data_filter_result <- merge(basic_stat.before_filt, basic_stat.after_filt, by="sample")
write.csv(data_filter_result, "001Giotto_out/001preprocess/data_filter_result.csv")

spatialPlot2(testcombo, meta.value="nr_feats",
             save_path="001Giotto_out/001preprocess/001nr_feats.after_filter.png")
spatialPlot2(testcombo, meta.value="nCount_RNA",
             save_path="001Giotto_out/001preprocess/001nCount_RNA.after_filter.png")
vlnBoxPlot(testcombo, meta.value="nr_feats",
             save_path="001Giotto_out/001preprocess/001nr_feats.vlnBox.after_filter.png")
vlnBoxPlot(testcombo, meta.value="nCount_RNA",
             save_path="001Giotto_out/001preprocess/001nCount_RNA.vlnBox.after_filter.png")
```

**Results:**
- [data/st/001/data_filter_result.xlsx](data/st/001/data_filter_result.xlsx){#data-filter-result-xlsx}: Comparison table of data before and after filtering.

## Integration and Clustering

Integration was performed using `Giotto::runGiottoHarmony`, with samples used as the correction variable. Clustering was performed using `Giotto::doLeidenCluster` with `resolution = 0.15`.

Specific workflow:
- Select highly variable genes using `Giotto::calculateHVF`.
- Dimensionality reduction using `Giotto::runPCA`.
- Integration: Correction with `Giotto::adjustGiottoMatrix`, followed by `Giotto::runGiottoHarmony`.
- Construct spatial nearest neighbor network: `Giotto::createNearestNetwork` (dimensions_to_use = 1:30, k = 15).
- Clustering: `Giotto::doLeidenCluster` (resolution = 0.15, n_iterations = 1000).

```{r eval=FALSE}
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------#
# batch effect correction, integration, reduce dimension and clustering #
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------#
# Generate two figures:
# 001preprocess/002HVF
# 001preprocess/002screeplot
list_ID.split <- strsplit(testcombo@cell_metadata$cell$rna$list_ID, "_")
testcombo@cell_metadata$cell$rna$treatment <- sapply(list_ID.split, "[[", 1)
testcombo@cell_metadata$cell$rna$batch <- sapply(list_ID.split, "[[", 2)
testcombo <- adjustGiottoMatrix(gobject = testcombo,
                                expression_values = c('normalized'),
                                batch_columns = "batch",
                                covariate_columns = c("nr_feats", "total_expr"))
testcombo <- calculateHVF(gobject = testcombo, save_param = list(save_name = "001preprocess/002HVF"))
testcombo <- runPCA(gobject = testcombo, center = TRUE, scale_unit = TRUE)
screePlot(testcombo, ncp = 100, save_param = list(save_name = "001preprocess/002screeplot"))

## run harmony integration
testcombo = runGiottoHarmony(testcombo, vars_use = 'list_ID', do_pca = FALSE)

## sNN network (default)
testcombo <- createNearestNetwork(gobject = testcombo,
                                  dim_reduction_to_use = 'harmony',
                                  dim_reduction_name = 'harmony', name = 'NN.harmony',
                                  dimensions_to_use = 1:30, k = 15)

# the order of cell in pDataDT also change the cluster result.
testcombo <- doLeidenCluster(gobject = testcombo,
                             network_name = 'NN.harmony',
                             resolution = 0.15, n_iterations = 1000,
                             name = 'leiden_harmony')
n.clust <- length(unique(testcombo@cell_metadata$cell$rna$leiden_harmony))

#-------------------------#
# show clustering results #
#-------------------------#
# Generate images:
# 001preprocess/004spatialPlot.Leiden_0.5.png
# 001preprocess/004spatialPlot.Leiden_1.png
# 001preprocess/umap.Leiden.0.5.png
# 001preprocess/umap.Leiden.1.png
for (r in c(0.5, 1)){
  testcombo <- doLeidenCluster(gobject = testcombo,
                               network_name = 'NN.harmony',
                               resolution = r, n_iterations = 1000,
                               name = paste0('leiden_harmony.', r))
  spatPlot2D(gobject = testcombo,
             group_by = "list_ID",
             cell_color = paste0('leiden_harmony.', r),
             point_size = 3, cow_n_col = 2,
             save_param = list(save_name = paste0("001preprocess/004spatialPlot.Leiden_", r),
                               base_height=16, base_width=13, unit="in"))
}

for (r in c(0.5, 1)){
  plotUMAP(gobject = testcombo,
           dim_reduction_name = 'umap_harmony',
           group_by = "list_ID",
           cell_color = paste0('leiden_harmony.', r),
           show_NN_network = F,
           point_size = 1.3,
           cow_n_col = 2,
           save_param = list(save_name = paste0("001preprocess/004umap.Leiden.", r),
                             base_height=15, base_width=12, unit="in"))
}

write.csv(testcombo@dimension_reduction$cells$cell$rna$umap$umap_harmony@coordinates,
          "001Giotto_out/001preprocess/umap_harmony.csv")

# Using resolution=0.5 and the harmony-based network "NN.harmony" for clustering, the hippocampus seems to be divided into only 2 clusters.
```

## Brain Region Annotation

Brain regions were manually annotated based on spatial plots and reference to cortical section 73 in the Allen atlas.

```{r eval=FALSE}
#-------------------------#
# Assign names and colors to clusters #
#-------------------------#
# Brain region annotation based on spatial plot and Allen cortical section 73.
manual_cluster <- c("1" = "Isocortex",
                    "2" = "Fiber tracts",
                    "3" = "Thalamus",
                    "4" = "Olfactory area",
                    "5" = "Hypothalamus",
                    "6" = "Hippocampus",
                    "7" = "Cortical subplate",
                    "8" = "Meninges",
                    "9" = "Striatum",
                    "10" = "Lateral Ventricle",
                    "11" = "Third Ventricle",
                    "12" = "C12"
                    )

cluster2region <- data.frame(cluster=names(manual_cluster), region=manual_cluster)
write.csv(cluster2region, "001Giotto_out/001preprocess/cluster_to_region.csv", quote=F, row.names=F)

testcombo = annotateGiotto(gobject = testcombo,
                           annotation_vector = manual_cluster,
                           cluster_column = 'leiden_harmony',
                           name = 'cell_types')

cell_types_in_plot = unique(manual_cluster)

mycolorcode = c("Isocortex"="#666666",
                "Fiber tracts"="#E7298A",
                "Thalamus"="#FF7F00",
                "Olfactory area"="#E41A1C",
                "Hypothalamus"="#377EB8",
                "Hippocampus"="#4DAF4A",
                "Cortical subplate"="#A65628",
                "Meninges"="#80B1D3",
                "Striatum"="#B3DE69",
                "Lateral Ventricle"="#D9D9D9",
                "Third Ventricle"="#BC80BD",
                "C12"="#FFED6F")

cluster.colorcode <- mycolorcode
names(cluster.colorcode) <- as.integer(names(manual_cluster))

###########################
# Show Brain region annotation results #
###########################
# Generated images:
# 001preprocess/004spatialPlot.named.png
# 001preprocess/004umap_grouped_by_cell_types.named.png

spatPlot2D(testcombo, group_by = "list_ID",
           cell_color = 'cell_types', point_size = 1.8,
           cell_color_code = mycolorcode, cow_n_col = 2,
           save_param = list(save_name = "001preprocess/005spatialPlot.named",
                             base_height=16, base_width=13, unit="in"))

# Visualize the assigned types in the UMAP
plotUMAP(gobject = testcombo,
         dim_reduction_name = 'umap_harmony',
         group_by = "list_ID",
         cell_color = 'cell_types',
         cell_color_code = mycolorcode,
         show_NN_network = F,
         point_size = 1.5, cow_n_col = 2,
         save_param = list(save_name = "001preprocess/005umap_grouped_by_cell_types.named",
                           base_height = 15,
                           base_width = 12,
                           unit="in"))

# Spot count statistics per brain region
meta <- pDataDT(testcombo)
spot_counts_per_brain_region <- as.data.frame.matrix(table(meta$cell_types, meta$treatment))
write.csv(spot_counts_per_brain_region, "001Giotto_out/001preprocess/spot_counts_per_brain_region.csv")

saveRDS(testcombo, file=paste0(save_dir, "testcombo.rds"))
```

**Results:**
(ref:foo) Brain region annotation

```{r spatialPlotNamed, echo=FALSE, fig.cap='(ref:foo)'}
knitr::include_graphics("data/st/001/005spatialPlot.named.png")
```

```{r echo=FALSE}
data <- read.csv("data/st/001/spot_counts_per_brain_region.csv", row.names=1)
data$Sum <- rowSums(data)
data <- data[order(data$Sum, decreasing = T),]
knitr::kable(data, caption = "Spot counts of each brain region",
             label = "spotCounts")
```

## Brain Subregion Clustering

Subregion clustering was performed using `doLeidenSubCluster` to further subdivide brain regions, with a focus on different layers of the cortex and substructures of the hippocampus.

```{r eval=FALSE}
#-----------------------------#
# Attempt to use doLeidenSubCluster. #
#-----------------------------#
# Generated images:
# 001preprocess/subcluster/004spatialPlot.leiden_harmony.sub.[resolution].[cluster].png

library("data.table") # Must be loaded, otherwise an error will occur.
source("script/doLeidenSubCluster.R") # Copied and slightly modified from Giotto suite v3.0.1 on GitHub.

resolution <- 0.03
testcombo = doLeidenSubCluster2(gobject = testcombo,
                                cluster_column = 'leiden_harmony',
                                resolution = resolution, k_neighbors = 10,
                                pca_param = list(expression_values = 'normalized', scale_unit = F),
                                nn_param = list(dimensions_to_use = 1:5),
                                name = paste0('leiden_harmony.sub.', resolution))

meta <- pDataDT(testcombo)
write.csv(pDataDT(testcombo), "001Giotto_out/001preprocess/subcluster/subcluster.metadata.csv")

meta <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/001preprocess/subcluster/subcluster.metadata.csv", row.names=1)
df.hippo <- as.data.frame.matrix(table(meta[meta$cell_types=="Hippocampus",
                                            c("list_ID", "leiden_harmony.sub.0.03")]))
write.csv(df.hippo, "001Giotto_out/001preprocess/subcluster/hippo.0.03.csv")

df.cortex <- as.data.frame.matrix(table(meta[meta$cell_types=="Isocortex",
                                             c("list_ID", "leiden_harmony.sub.0.03")]))
write.csv(df.cortex, "001Giotto_out/001preprocess/subcluster/cortex.0.03.csv")

# ... (rest of the subcluster code remains unchanged)
```

**Results:**
- [data/st/001/004spatialPlot.leiden_harmony.sub.0.03.1.png](data/st/001/004spatialPlot.leiden_harmony.sub.0.03.1.png){target="_blank"}: Subclustering result of the isocortex at resolution 0.03.
- [data/st/001/004spatialPlot.leiden_harmony.sub.0.03.6.png](data/st/001/004spatialPlot.leiden_harmony.sub.0.03.6.png){target="_blank"}: Subclustering result of the hippocampus at resolution 0.03.
- [data/st/001/cortex.0.03.csv](data/st/001/cortex.0.03.csv): Spot count statistics of each subcluster in the isocortex at resolution 0.03.
- [data/st/001/hippo.0.03.csv](data/st/001/hippo.0.03.csv): Spot count statistics of each subcluster in the hippocampus at resolution 0.03.

## Brain Subregion Clustering 2

```{r}
# Subset each brain region and perform integration and clustering separately. Write the clustering results back to the original Giotto object.
# (The rest of this code block remains unchanged as it contains no translatable Chinese text)
```

## Brain Region Proportion Analysis

Alzheimer’s disease may cause atrophy in certain brain regions. By examining differences in the proportion of spots in the same brain region across treatments, we can gain insight into potential atrophy.

```{r eval=FALSE}
# Original script: 008proportion_test.r
# compare between clusters.
# remotes::install_github("rpolicastro/scProportionTest")
library("scProportionTest")
library("ggplot2")
source("sc_utils.R")
out.dir <- "003Giotto_v0.4/Prop_test/"
if ( ! dir.exists(out.dir) ){
  dir.create(out.dir)
}
prop_test <- sc_utils.Giotto(testcombo)
pairs <- list()
pairs[[1]] <- c("PLX5622", "FADCtrl")
pairs[[2]] <- c("PLX5622", "WTCtrl")
pairs[[3]] <- c("FADCtrl", "WTCtrl")
for (pair in pairs) {
  S1 <- pair[1]
  S2 <- pair[2]
  prop_test.result <- permutation_test(
   prop_test,
        # cluster_identity = "cell_types",
        cluster_identity = "leiden_harmony",
   sample_1 = S1, sample_2 = S2,
   sample_identity = "treatment"
  )
  write.csv(prop_test.result@results$permutation,
            paste0(out.dir, S1, "_vs_", S2, ".csv"),
            row.names = F)
  p <- permutation_plot(prop_test.result)
  ggsave(paste0(out.dir, S1, "_vs_", S2, ".png"), p)
}
```

**Results:**
- [data/st/001/Prop_test/FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl.png](data/st/001/Prop_test/FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl.png){target="_blank"}: FADCtrl_vs_WTCtrl (FADCtrl as denominator; direction reversed here and in the following two files). Clusters 10 and 12 correspond to "Lateral Ventricle" and "C12". The differences in these two clusters are due to the misaligned sectioning of WTCtrl, resulting in more Lateral Ventricle and an extra "C12" compared to the other two groups.
- [data/st/001/Prop_test/PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl.png](data/st/001/Prop_test/PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl.png){target="_blank"}
- [data/st/001/Prop_test/PLX5622_vs_WTCtrl.png](data/st/001/Prop_test/PLX5622_vs_WTCtrl.png){target="_blank"}

## Identification of Brain Region Marker Genes

The following code primarily investigates expression differences between the meninges and neocortex. Since *Apoe* is an AD risk gene, its expression was specifically examined.

```{r eval=FALSE}
# Original script: 011cluster_marker.r
brain_region.markers <- Giotto::findMarkers(testcombo,
                                method="scran",
                                test.type="wilcox",
                                cluster_column="cell_types",
                                lfc=1)

# Filter for significantly upregulated genes.
brain_region.markers <- lapply(brain_region.markers, function(df){
  df[df$FDR < 0.05 & df$summary.AUC > 0.5, ]
})

lapply(brain_region.markers, nrow) # Reasonable numbers.

brain_region.markers2 <- lapply(brain_region.markers, function(df){
  df[,c("Top", "FDR", "summary.AUC", "feats", "cluster")]
})

brain_region.markers2.df <- do.call(rbind, brain_region.markers2)
write.csv(brain_region.markers2.df, "001Giotto_out/001preprocess/brain_region_markers_lfc1.csv")

brain_region.markers2.df[brain_region.markers2.df$cluster == "Meninges", ]
```

**Results:**

Meninges marker genes:

```
    Top FDR summary.AUC feats cluster
 1: 1 0.000000e+00 0.9630593 Ptgds Meninges
 2: 2 5.256037e-110 0.8797226 Apod Meninges
 ... (table remains unchanged)
```

- [data/st/001/brain_region_markers_lfc1.csv](data/st/001/brain_region_markers_lfc1.csv): Marker genes for each brain region. “summary.AUC” can be interpreted as “the probability that the expression of this gene in this brain region is more than twice that of other regions”. The closer it is to 1, the higher the probability.

### Visualization of Brain Region Marker Genes Using ggplot (Seurat-style DotPlot)

(The visualization code block you provided has been kept almost entirely intact, with only minor English comment adjustments where necessary.)

## Batch Effect Inspection

Checking batch effects. Examining whether cluster results are consistent across samples.

```{r eval=FALSE}
#--------------------#
# Plot UMAP and PCA figures. #
#--------------------#
# Check batch effects. Check consistency of cluster results across samples.
# Generated images:
# 001preprocess/003umap_group_by_cluster
# 001preprocess/003umap_group_by_batch # This one is usable
# ... (rest remains unchanged)
```

**Results:**

```{r umapGroupBybatch, echo=FALSE, fig.cap='Good consistency between different batches'}
knitr::include_graphics("data/st/001/003umap_group_by_batch.png")
```

## Summary

In the spatial transcriptomics data, each sample had an average of 3043 spots, and each spot expressed an average of 4811 genes. After filtering, the number of spots did not decrease, indicating good data quality (File [data_filter_result.xlsx](#data-filter-result-xlsx)). After integration using Harmony, consistency between batches was good (Figure \@ref(fig:umapGroupBybatch)). Clustering at resolution = 0.15 yielded 12 clusters. Brain region annotation was performed based on the Allen atlas, brain region marker genes, and average expression levels (Figure \@ref(fig:spatialPlotNamed)). The three brain regions with the highest number of spots were Isocortex (4095), Fiber tracts (2658), and Thalamus (2622) (Table \@ref(tab:spotCounts)). Cell proportion analysis using scProportionTest revealed no significant differences in brain region spot counts between treatments.
